target.technique_summary: This is an instructional video demonstrating laparoscopic suturing techniques rather than an actual FLS Task 5 performance. While Teacher B identified some needle loading activity in the first 18 seconds, both teachers agreed no complete task execution occurred. The maximum penalty is applied as no meaningful task completion was achieved. Disagreement resolved by favoring Claude's assessment that this is purely educational content.
improvement_suggestions: This is an educational video, not a task performance
To score FLS Task 5, need video of actual task execution with complete suturing and knot tying
strengths: Good instructional details on physical principles

target.technique_summary: Solid performance with proper surgeon's knot followed by appropriate single throws with hand switching. Good suture placement accuracy and complete drain closure. Minor disagreement resolved on phase timings (Teacher A included idle phase, Teacher B had extended suture cut phase) and suture placement deviations (averaged between 3.5mm and 2.5mm total). Both teachers agreed on excellent knot technique with proper hand switching.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise targeting of marks to minimize deviation penalties
Maintain consistent speed while transitioning between phases
strengths: Good knot security and tension maintenance

target.technique_summary: Solid performance with proper knot tying sequence, appropriate hand switching between throws, and good suture placement. Teachers disagreed on completion time (132s vs 147s) and suture placement penalties (3.5mm vs 2.5mm). Favored Claude's more detailed timing analysis while taking middle ground on placement accuracy. Both agreed on excellent knot technique and complete slit closure.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to reduce overall completion time
Focus on more precise suture placement to minimize deviation penalties
Enhance precision during initial needle placement
strengths: Excellent knot tying technique with proper surgeon's knot

target.technique_summary: Efficient intracorporeal knot tying demonstration with proper surgeon's knot and hand alternation. Teachers disagreed on phase timing details (Claude: 42s total vs GPT-4o: 22s total) and single vs multi-throw classification for throws 2&3. Resolved by taking GPT-4o's more precise total time while adopting Claude's more detailed throw analysis. Both agreed on excellent technique quality.
improvement_suggestions: Continue to enhance economy of motion
Maintain consistent tension during knot tightening
strengths: Excellent demonstration of surgeon's knot technique
Good instrument coordination and secure knot construction

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well closed with no visible gaps

target.technique_summary: This video demonstrates proper FLS Task 5 technique with appropriate surgeon's knot followed by single throws with hand switching. There was disagreement between evaluators on hand switching pattern (Claude: R-L-R vs GPT-4o: R-R-L) and suture placement accuracy (Claude: 4mm total deviation vs GPT-4o: 1.5mm). Consensus favors moderate placement accuracy and follows Claude's hand switching assessment as it shows more detailed alternation pattern. Total completion time of 147 seconds used from GPT-4o as it matches phase timing summation.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise suture placement to minimize deviation from marks
Maintain consistent tension throughout the knot tying process
Ensure consistent traction during knot tightening
